task: I’m planning a cozy Christmas day at home with my toddler and want ideas for both of us that I can actually look at on screen afterward. Could you start on Hobbycraft and find three low-mess Christmas craft activities that feel toddler-friendly, then open the actual project pages in separate tabs so I can compare the photos and note the materials each one needs? After that, use Google to find three more at-home Christmas activity ideas for a toddler that are clearly different from the Hobbycraft ones, because I want a fuller mix of options beyond simple repeats, and open at least one of those source pages so I can see it’s a real activity page. Once you’ve got the toddler plan sorted, switch over to Scratch Magazine and look for Grinch Christmas nail inspiration, then find one Grinch tutorial page I could realistically follow and leave that tutorial page open so I can check the design details myself. From there, go to Amazon and find at least three Grinch-themed nail art ideas or products that use different materials, like stickers or decals, gel polish, brushes, glitter, rhinestones, stamping plates, or similar, and make sure they actually fit the style or techniques shown on the Scratch tutorial. Please open the most promising Amazon product pages in their own tabs so I can compare them visually, and then give me a clear summary with the toddler activity options, the Scratch tutorial, and the three nail product ideas with links plus a quick note on how each one matches the Grinch look.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent identified three Hobbycraft projects: '12 Days of FREE Christmas Activity Downloads', 'How to Make a Snowman Jar', and '4 Quick Christmas Projects to Make with Kids'. It summarized the materials for each in the final response and opened the corresponding pages.

R2: success - The agent found three distinct non-Hobbycraft activities (scavenger hunt, story basket, and baking) and opened the source page 'https://rainydaymum.co.uk/christmas-activities-for-toddlers/'. These are clearly different from the craft-focused Hobbycraft activities.

R3: success - The agent navigated to and left open the Scratch Magazine Grinch tutorial at 'https://www.scratchmagazine.co.uk/nail_art/the-grinch-christmas-nail-tutorial/'. The final response identifies this URL and describes the tutorial content.

R4: success - The agent opened three Amazon product pages: Grinch nail stickers/decals, a gel liner polish set, and a fine liner nail art brush set. These represent different material types (stickers, gel polish, and brushes) as requested.

R5: success - The final summary includes all required elements: the three Hobbycraft activities with materials, three additional Google-sourced ideas, the Scratch tutorial link, and three Amazon product links with notes on how they match the Grinch tutorial techniques (e.g., using the gel liner set for the fine details described in the tutorial).

rubric_results.requirement: Exactly three low-mess toddler-friendly Christmas craft activities are identified on Hobbycraft, with materials summarized for each.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm three distinct Hobbycraft activity pages were opened or visited and the final response includes each activity name plus its materials list.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent identified three Hobbycraft projects: '12 Days of FREE Christmas Activity Downloads', 'How to Make a Snowman Jar', and '4 Quick Christmas Projects to Make with Kids'. It summarized the materials for each in the final response and opened the corresponding pages.

rubric_results.requirement: At least three additional at-home toddler Christmas activity ideas are found via Google and are different from the Hobbycraft activities, each with a short description.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm Google search results were used, at least one non-Hobbycraft source page was opened, and the final response lists three distinct additional ideas that do not duplicate the Hobbycraft ones.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent found three distinct non-Hobbycraft activities (scavenger hunt, story basket, and baking) and opened the source page 'https://rainydaymum.co.uk/christmas-activities-for-toddlers/'. These are clearly different from the craft-focused Hobbycraft activities.

rubric_results.requirement: Grinch Christmas nail inspiration is found on Scratch Magazine, including one specific Grinch tutorial page title and URL.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm navigation on Scratch Magazine to a Grinch-related nail page and that a tutorial page was left open or clearly identified with title and link.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent navigated to and left open the Scratch Magazine Grinch tutorial at 'https://www.scratchmagazine.co.uk/nail_art/the-grinch-christmas-nail-tutorial/'. The final response identifies this URL and describes the tutorial content.

rubric_results.requirement: At least three Amazon Grinch-themed nail art ideas or products are found using different material types, such as decals, gel polish, glitter, brushes, rhinestones, or stamping plates.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm three Amazon product pages or listings were opened or visited, with products spanning different materials such as decals, gel, glitter, brushes, rhinestones, or stamping tools.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent opened three Amazon product pages: Grinch nail stickers/decals, a gel liner polish set, and a fine liner nail art brush set. These represent different material types (stickers, gel polish, and brushes) as requested.

rubric_results.requirement: The final summary ties the Amazon products back to the Scratch tutorial style or techniques and includes all toddler activities, the tutorial, and product links.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the final response contains the three Hobbycraft crafts with materials, three additional Google-sourced toddler ideas with descriptions, one Scratch tutorial title and URL, and three Amazon product ideas with links plus notes explaining how they match the Scratch inspiration.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The final summary includes all required elements: the three Hobbycraft activities with materials, three additional Google-sourced ideas, the Scratch tutorial link, and three Amazon product links with notes on how they match the Grinch tutorial techniques (e.g., using the gel liner set for the fine details described in the tutorial).
